title: odontites serotinus subsp. vernus
intro: Odontites serotinus subsp. vernus, also known as the early odontites or green mouse-ear, is a plant species belonging to the family Orobanchaceae, characterized by its green, mouse-ear-shaped leaves and small, white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. It has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion, making it a valuable remedy for gastrointestinal issues and other conditions. Herbal preparations of Odontites serotinus subsp. vernus can be found in the form of infusions, tinctures, and teas, often used to treat digestive problems and promote overall well-being.
